1.gl: supreme in authority, i.e. (as noun) controller; by implication, Master (as a respectful title)
figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
1.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

1.gl: a calling out, i.e. (concretely) a popular meeting, especially a religious congregation (Jewish synagogue, or Christian community of members on earth or saints in heaven or both)
1.dv: from a compound of G1537 (ἐκ) and a derivative of G2564 (καλέω);

2.gl: supreme in authority, i.e. (as noun) controller; by implication, Master (as a respectful title)
figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
2.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

2.gl: graciousness (as gratifying), of manner or act (abstract or concrete; literal, figurative or spiritual; especially the divine influence upon the heart, and its reflection in the life; including gratitude)
2.kj: acceptable, benefit, favour, gift, grace(- ious), joy, liberality, pleasure, thank(-s, -worthy)

2.gl: peace (literally or figuratively); by implication, prosperity
2.kj: one, peace, quietness, rest, + set at one again
2.dv: probably from a primary verb (to join);

3.gl: figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
3.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

3.gl: persuasion, i.e. credence; moral conviction (of religious truth, or the truthfulness of God or a religious teacher), especially reliance upon Christ for salvation; abstractly, constancy in such profession; by extension,

4.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

4.gl: glory (as very apparent), in a wide application (literal or figurative, objective or subjective)

6.gl: equitable (in character or act); by implication, innocent, holy (absolutely or relatively)
